description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular, single-story agricultural building with a prominent gable roof clad in reddish-brown tiles. The structure is supported by wooden posts and features a combination of open bays and enclosed sections, with the central area partially walled and fitted with a large sliding barn door. The exterior walls are clad in light tan wooden planks, and the open bays on either side provide easy access and ventilation for livestock. The roof overhangs the structure, offering additional shelter along the sides. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and airflow, with minimal decorative elements.
description: A rectangular cowshed with a reddish-brown gable roof, open bays, and a central sliding barn door, built from light tan wood.
description_view_0: Front view showing the long side with open bays, a central sliding barn door, and supporting wooden posts.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the triangular gable end, roof pitch, and two open bays beneath the roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the opposite long side, mostly enclosed with a large central door and fewer open bays.
description_view_3: Opposite end view showing the gable end and a single open bay, with minimal wall detailing.
attribute_door_style: sliding barn door, wooden construction with horizontal planks
attribute_window_presence: no windows, open bays on sides for light and access
